skill.name: turkce-kod
skill.description: Türkçe yazılım projeleri için kod dokümantasyonu, yorum satırları, README ve commit mesajları yazma rehberi. Teknik terimlerin Türkçe karşılıkları ve Türk geliştirici topluluğu standartları.
skill.summary: Türkçe yazılım projeleri için tutarlı kod dokümantasyonu ve commit mesajları yazma rehberi
skill.author: cesareth

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 75 'external_commands' patterns and 3 'weak cryptographic' patterns in SKILL.md, all of which are FALSE POSITIVES. These detections are backtick characters (`) within markdown code blocks - they are documentation examples showing Turkish code comments, not executable code. The skill contains only markdown documentation with no executable scripts, network requests, or actual code execution. No security risks found.

content.value_statement: Maintains consistent Turkish documentation standards across software projects. Provides templates for code comments, commit messages, and README files in Turkish.
actual_capabilities: Generates Turkish code comments following proper formatting conventions
Writes structured Turkish commit messages with type prefixes (ekle, düzelt, güncelle)
Creates Turkish README.md templates with installation and quick start sections
Translates technical terminology to Turkish with consistent vocabulary
Provides Turkish error messages and logging patterns
Advises on variable naming conventions for Turkish projects
limitations: Only supports Turkish language documentation - does not translate other languages
Does not execute code or modify existing files automatically
Requires user to manually copy generated templates into their projects
Technical term translations follow Turkish developer community standards, which may vary by region

use_cases.title: New Turkish Project Setup
use_cases.description: Developer starting a new Turkish-language software project needs README.md, code comment standards, and commit message conventions.
use_cases.target_user: Turkish software developers starting new projects

use_cases.title: Team Documentation Standardization
use_cases.description: Development team wants to enforce consistent Turkish documentation across multiple contributors and repositories.
use_cases.target_user: Tech leads managing Turkish development teams

use_cases.title: Codebase Localization
use_cases.description: Existing English codebase needs translation to Turkish with proper terminology and formatting.
use_cases.target_user: Developers localizing international projects for Turkish users

prompt_templates.title: Generate Function Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Turkish code comments for this function following the style guide: explain what the function does, its parameters, return value, and any exceptions it raises.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has written a function and needs professional Turkish documentation

prompt_templates.title: Create Commit Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Turkish commit message for this change using the format '<tür>: <açıklama>'. Choose an appropriate type (ekle, düzelt, güncelle, sil, yeniden-düzenle) and write a clear description under 72 characters.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has made code changes and needs a properly formatted Turkish commit message

prompt_templates.title: Build README Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Turkish README.md template for a [project type] project. Include sections for installation, quick start with code examples, features list, configuration table, and contribution guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: User is starting a new project needs a complete Turkish README structure

prompt_templates.title: Translate Technical Terms
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code and suggest Turkish translations for variable names and comments while keeping framework-specific terms in English. Maintain consistency with the Turkish developer community standards.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to localize code comments and variable names to Turkish

output_examples.input: Write a Turkish commit message for adding user authentication
output_examples.output:
ekle: kullanıcı kimlik doğrulama sistemi

JWT tabanlı oturum yönetimi eklendi. Şifre hashing bcrypt kullanılarak yapıldı. Oturum süresi 24 saat olarak ayarlandı.

Refs: #123

output_examples.input: Generate Turkish documentation for a function that calculates cart total
output_examples.output:
/**
 * Sepetteki ürünlerin toplam fiyatını hesaplar.
 * KDV dahil tutar için kdvDahilFiyat fonksiyonunu kullan.
 *
 * @param urunler - Fiyat bilgisi içeren ürün dizisi
 * @returns Toplam fiyat (kuruş cinsinden, tam sayı)
 */

output_examples.input: Create a Turkish README structure for a REST API project
output_examples.output:
# Proje Adı

Projenin ne yaptığını tek cümlede açıkla.

## Kurulum

```bash
npm install proje-adi
```

## Hızlı Başlangıç

## Özellikler

## Yapılandırma

## Katkı Sağlama

## Lisans

best_practices: Write code comments explaining why code exists, not what it does - the code itself shows what
Keep variable names consistent throughout the project - choose either Turkish or English and maintain it
Use structured commit message format with type prefixes for better git history tracking
Include parameter types, return values, and raised exceptions in function documentation
anti_patterns: Mixing Turkish and English variable names within the same file or module
Writing cryptic comments that repeat the code without adding context
Using generic commit messages like 'update' or 'fix bug' without specific details
Writing comments in English while the rest of the project documentation is in Turkish

faq.question: Should I use Turkish or English for variable names?
faq.answer: Follow the project's existing convention. For new projects, use Turkish for domain logic (musteri, siparis, fatura) but keep framework terms in English (request, response, callback). Maintain consistency throughout the codebase.

faq.question: Can I use Turkish characters (ş, ı, ğ) in variable names?
faq.answer: While Python 3 and modern JavaScript support Turkish characters, prefer ASCII equivalents (s instead of ş, i instead of ı) to avoid terminal compatibility issues. Use musteri_adi instead of müşteri_adı.

faq.question: What commit message types should I use?
faq.answer: Use ekle for new features, düzelt for bug fixes, güncelle for improvements, sil for deletions, yeniden-düzenle for refactoring, test for tests, belge for documentation, and yapılandır for configuration changes.

faq.question: How detailed should code comments be?
faq.answer: Focus on explaining the why, not the what. Complex business logic, non-obvious decisions, and workarounds need comments. Simple code that speaks for itself does not. Always document function parameters, return values, and exceptions.

faq.question: Should I translate technical terms to Turkish?
faq.answer: Keep well-established technical terms in English (authentication, middleware, endpoint). Refer to the technical terms reference for translations. The goal is clarity, not forced translation of standard terminology.

faq.question: Can this skill write comments for any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides documentation templates and examples for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, and Go. The principles apply to any language - you can adapt the comment syntax to your preferred programming language.
